task_completion.visual_evidence: The main menu or window of the GCompris educational software is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the 'Left and Right' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Left and Right' activity interface is visible, typically showing a hand or object and buttons to select 'left' or 'right'.

task_completion.description: Identify the first displayed hand or object.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks or selects the correct left or right button corresponding to the displayed hand or object.

task_completion.description: Continue identifying hands or objects to progress through the level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Subsequent screenshots show different hands or objects being displayed and identified, or a progress bar/indicator advancing.

task_completion.description: Finish the level and trigger the success indicator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A victory or congratulations animation is displayed on the screen (e.g., flowers, penguins, or text saying 'Congratulations' or 'Well Done').

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of GCompris.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse clicks and cursor movements are visible on the GCompris UI elements, rather than terminal commands solving the task programmatically.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual GCompris application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic GCompris application window and interface, not a web browser searching for images or a text editor.

integrity.description: The success screen is generated by the game, not loaded from an external image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The congratulations animation appears naturally within the GCompris application window context, without image viewer UI or web browser frames around it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target activity in GCompris is named 'leftright'. Successful completion of the activity is indicated by a built-in congratulations animation (Bonus.qml component), which provides visual feedback such as flowers, penguins, or text like 'Congratulations' or 'Well Done'.
